info.title: Webhook Debugger & Logger API
info.description: OpenAPI description for the Webhook Debugger & Logger Actor web server. Authentication is configuration-driven: when authKey is configured, management routes require either a bearer token or the key query parameter; when authKey is unset, those routes remain accessible without credentials.

tags.name: Replay
tags.description: Replay captured webhook traffic to a new target

tags.name: Streaming
tags.description: Server-Sent Events stream of live webhook ingestion

get.description: Returns the dashboard HTML page. If the Accept header includes text/plain, the route returns a compact plain-text summary instead.

text/plain.example:
Webhook Debugger & Logger (v3.0.0)
Active Webhooks: 1
Signature Verification: STRIPE

post.description: Captures incoming webhook traffic. The Express route accepts any HTTP method; this OpenAPI operation documents the common POST workflow. If authKey is configured, this route also accepts bearer token or key query authentication.

200.description: Captured successfully using default or custom response behavior

get.description: Returns runtime metadata, active webhook state, discoverable endpoints, and the feature list.
Queries captured webhook events from the DuckDB read model using offset-based or cursor-based pagination.
Returns a single log entry. Use the optional fields parameter for sparse responses.
Returns the original captured payload. If the payload was offloaded to Apify KVS, it is hydrated on demand.

200.description: Original payload in its stored form

post.description: Replays a captured webhook event to a new destination URL after SSRF and DNS validation.

get.description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for live webhook monitoring. The current implementation broadcasts plain data frames and keepalive comments rather than named event frames.
Returns sync-service metrics for the Dataset-to-DuckDB replication loop.
Liveness probe for container and uptime monitoring.
Readiness probe for orchestrators and load balancers.

bearerAuth.description: Use the configured authKey as a bearer token when authKey protection is enabled.

queryKeyAuth.name: key
queryKeyAuth.description: Use the configured authKey in the key query parameter when authKey protection is enabled.

ItemId.name: itemId
ItemId.description: Log ID to replay. If it parses as a timestamp and no log ID is found, the server attempts a timestamp fallback lookup.

ReplayUrl.name: url
ReplayUrl.description: Replay destination URL. The server validates it against SSRF and DNS safety rules.

ForcedStatus.name: __status
ForcedStatus.description: Override the response status code for the current webhook request.

Sort.name: sort
Sort.description: Comma-separated sort rules such as timestamp:desc,method:asc.
